Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given an event log for 5 cases tickets 101 to 105 The task is to 1 Identify cases with significantly longer total resolution times 2 Determine potential root causes of the performance issues 3 Explain how these factors lead to increased cycle times and propose insights or recommendations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First we need to calculate the total resolution time for each case The resolution time is the time from Receive Ticket to Resolve Ticket Note that the last event is Close Ticket but the resolution happens at Resolve Ticket So we will measure from the first event Receive Ticket to the event Resolve Ticket

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that the problem says the first event of each ticket marks when the ticket was received and the last event marks the closure of the ticket But for resolution time we are interested in the time until the ticket is resolved which is the Resolve Ticket activity The closing is a subsequent step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: So for each case we will Find the timestamp of the first event Receive Ticket Find the timestamp of the Resolve Ticket event Calculate the difference in time in hours or days between these two

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets extract the events for each case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 101 Receive Ticket 2024 03 01 08 00 Resolve Ticket 2024 03 01 10 00 Resolution time 2 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 102 Receive Ticket 2024 03 01 08 05 Resolve Ticket 2024 03 02 09 00 Resolution time 25 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 103 Receive Ticket 2024 03 01 08 10 Resolve Ticket 2024 03 01 09 15 Resolution time 1 hour 5 minutes approx 1 083 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 104 Receive Ticket 2024 03 01 08 20 Resolve Ticket 2024 03 02 08 00 Resolution time 23 hours 40 minutes approx 23 67 hours

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 105 Receive Ticket 2024 03 01 08 25 Resolve Ticket 2024 03 03 09 00 Resolution time 48 hours 35 minutes approx 48 58 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now lets list the resolution times in hours Case 101 2 hours Case 102 25 hours Case 103 1 08 hours Case 104 23 67 hours Case 105 48 58 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We can see that Cases 102 104 and 105 are taking much longer than the average

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The average resolution time is 20 066 hours So Cases 102 104 and 105 are above average

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the distribution Cases 101 and 103 are very quick around 1 to 2 hours Cases 102 104 and 105 are taking 25 24 and 48 hours respectively So all three are significantly longer

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 105 is the longest then Case 102 then Case 104

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now we need to determine potential root causes We are to consider Presence of escalations Long waiting times between activities Unnecessary delays before investigation and resolution steps

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 101 no escalation Total time 2 hours

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 102 Escalate to Level 2 Agent after assignment Then investigate later and resolve next day So for Case 102 we have Escalation to Level 2 Long wait after escalation before investigation started Then a very long gap from investigation to resolution

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 103 Quick no escalation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 104 Long wait before investigation 3 5 hours after assignment Then a long gap from investigation to resolution No escalation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 105 Escalation to Level 2 After escalation there was a long wait from 10 00 day1 to 14 00 day2 before the Level 2 agent started investigation Then the investigation took 19 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Escalations Cases 102 and 105 had escalations Case 104 did not But Case 104 also had a long resolution time So escalation is a factor but not the only one

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Long waiting times In Case 102 there is a wait after assignment and a wait after escalation In Case 104 there is a 3 5 hour wait before investigation In Case 105 there is a 28 hour wait after escalation before investigation

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Potential root causes Escalations Cases that escalate take longer However escalation might be necessary for complex issues The process after escalation seems to have long delays waiting for Level 2 agent to start and then long investigation times

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource unavailability In Case 102 after escalation the Level 2 agent started investigation only after 2 5 hours That might be because the Level 2 agent was busy In Case 105 the Level 2 agent started investigation after 28 hours It could be that the Level 2 agent was not available or the ticket was not prioritized

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Long investigation times The investigation step in the delayed cases took 19 hours This could be due to the complexity of the issue

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: But also might be because the agent did not work on it continuously Or the issue required more research However without more data we note that the investigation step in the quick cases was short

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Delays between steps There are significant waiting times between activities For example in Case 104 after assignment they waited 3 5 hours before starting investigation Maybe the agent was busy with other tickets

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Or the customer did not provide enough information But the log does not show any such activity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The main factors are Escalations in Cases 102 and 105 and the subsequent long delays in starting investigation by Level 2 agents Long waiting times between activities especially after assignment and after escalation Long investigation times 19 hours in the delayed cases

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The process after escalation seems to have long delays We should ensure that when a ticket is escalated it is immediately assigned to an available Level 2 agent In Case 105 the wait was 28 hours We need to set a service level agreement for escalation response time

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The long waits might be due to unavailability of agents We should monitor the workload of agents and ensure that there is enough coverage especially for Level 2 agents who are specialized Maybe we need more Level 2 agents or better distribution of tickets

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The long investigation times might be reduced by better training of Level 1 agents so that they can handle more complex issues without escalation Also providing better knowledge base or tools might help agents resolve issues faster

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We should monitor the time between steps and set alerts if a ticket is waiting too long at any step For example if a ticket is assigned and the agent hasnt started investigation within 30 minutes an alert should be triggered

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The cases with significantly longer resolution times are 102 25 hours 104 24 hours and 105 49 hours
